title: Starbucks Recommendation Engine
subtitle: Content-based drink recommendation and new product optimization model
description:
## Model Summary

A content-based recommendation engine for Starbucks beverages that provides:
1. **Personalized drink recommendations** based on customer persona, weather, time of day, and budget
2. **New product design optimization** for seasonal Frappuccino launches
3. **Customization upsell suggestions** to maximize average ticket size

## How It Works

### Product Scorer
Scores every menu item against persona constraints (max calories, min caffeine, budget) and context (temperature, time slot). Scoring weights:
- Category fit: 30 pts
- Temperature fit: 20 pts
- Time-of-day fit: 15 pts
- Value score: 20 pts
- Seasonal bonus: 15 pts

### Product Optimizer
Uses `scipy.optimize.minimize` with L-BFGS-B to find optimal product attributes:
- Score = Desirability (0.35) × Affordability (0.35) × Seasonal Fit (0.30)
- Constraints: Calories 150-500, Sugar 20-65g, Caffeine 0-200mg, Price $4-$7.50

### 5 Customer Personas
| Persona | Max Cal | Min Caffeine | Max Price | Custom Budget |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Morning Commuter | 400 | 75mg | $6.00 | $1.50 |
| Afternoon Treat | 600 | 0mg | $7.50 | $2.50 |
| Health Conscious | 200 | 0mg | $6.50 | $1.00 |
| Student | 500 | 50mg | $5.50 | $1.00 |
| Weekend Explorer | 500 | 0mg | $7.00 | $2.00 |

## Usage

```python
import pickle
with open('recommendation_engine.pkl', 'rb') as f:
    model = pickle.load(f)

# Access persona constraints
print(model['personas']['morning_commuter'])

# Access optimizer bounds
print(model['components']['product_optimizer']['bounds'])
```

## Related Notebooks
- [Menu EDA](https://www.kaggle.com/code/shiratoriseto/starbucks-menu-eda-nutrition-pricing-analysis)
- [New Frappuccino Optimizer](https://www.kaggle.com/code/shiratoriseto/starbucks-new-frappuccino-design-optimizer)
- [Personalized Recommendation Engine](https://www.kaggle.com/code/shiratoriseto/starbucks-personalized-recommendation-engine)
- [Validation & Stress Test](https://www.kaggle.com/code/shiratoriseto/starbucks-validation-stress-test)

## Limitations
- Scoring weights are hand-tuned, not learned from real data
- No collaborative filtering (intentional — avoids circular reasoning with synthetic data)
- Customization suggestions are rule-based
- With real POS data, weights could be optimized via A/B testing
overview: Personalized Starbucks drink recommendation and new Frappuccino design optimization
